name: omarchy_manual_installation
content:
Manua instaation 
If you can't use the Omarchy ISO, you can do a manua instaation using the vania arch
ISO, archinsta, and foowing the steps in this guide. This is not something most peope
shoud attempt, but if you know what you're doing, and why you're doing it, this is how.
�Downoad the Arch Linux ISO, put it on a USB stick (use baenaEtcher on Mac/
Windows), and boot off the stick (remember to turn off Secure Boot in the BIOS!.
�If you're on wifi, start by running iwctl , then type station wlan0 scan , then
station wlan0 connect <tab> , pick your network, and enter the password. If you're
on ethernet, you don't need this.
�Run archinstall  and pick these options (and eave anything not mentioned as-is):
Section Option
Mirrors and repositories Seect regions > Your country
Disk configuration Partitioning > Defaut partitioning ayout > Seect disk
(with space + return)
Disk > Fie system btrfs (defaut structure: yes + use compression)
Disk > Disk encryption Encryption type: LUKS  Encryption password +
Partitions (seect the one)
Hostname Give your computer a name
Bootoader Limine
Authentication > Root
password Set yours
Authentication > User
account Add a user > Superuser: Yes > Confirm and exit
Appications > Audio pipewire
Network configuration Copy ISO network config
Timezone Set yours
Beware that you must setup disk encryption to use Omarchy as designed! The setup reies
excusivey on disk encryption to secure your device, as it' auto-ogin the user after the
disk has been decrypted at boot.
Just note that this encryption setup won't aow you to enter the password from a
Buetooth keyboard at startup. Just ike you can't use a Buetooth keyboard to enter the
BIOS on a PC. You' need a keyboard that either uses a 2.4ghz donge or a cabe (which is
much nicer for atency anyway!. I personay ove the Lofree Fow84!
Here's what the disk encryption setup shoud ook ike. You need to pick LUKS, then set

---

Here's what the disk encryption setup shoud ook ike. You need to pick LUKS, then set
the encryption password, then appy to the partition (this step is crucia or nothing gets
encrypted!
Once Arch has been instaed, pick reboot, ogin with the user you just setup, and now
you're ready to insta Omarchy by running:
curl -fsSL https:��omarchy.org/install | bash
It' first ask you to sudo, then shorty thereafter, it' ask for your name and emai address.
Those credentias are used to preconfigure git (git config ��global user.name/email)
and set for auto-expansion on CapsLock Space E (emai) and CapsLock Space N (name).
After that, it' run by itsef for 530 minutes, depending on the speed of your internet
connection. When it's a done, it' ask for your permission to reboot the system.
Now you're ready to Omarchy!
